steps.title: Identify the grammatical structure around the blank
steps.content: The sentence is "The smoke point of corn oil ______ around 450 degrees Fahrenheit." The blank falls between the linking verb "is" and its subject complement "around 450 degrees Fahrenheit." A linking verb connects directly to its complement — no punctuation belongs between them.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — is—: an em dash signals a dramatic break or interruption; a plain complement does not warrant one. ✗
  • Choice B — is;: a semicolon must join two independent clauses; "around 450 degrees Fahrenheit" is a phrase, not a clause. ✗
  • Choice C — is: no punctuation; the linking verb flows cleanly into its complement. ✓
  • Choice D — is,: a comma between a linking verb and its complement violates standard grammar. ✗
That confirms C is correct.
